title: Global citizenship and sustainable development education
subtitle: An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.
caption:
Global citizenship and sustainable development education

An index from 0 (worst) to 1 (best) measuring the extent to which global citizenship and sustainable development education are mainstreamed into different strands of education: national education policies, student assessment, teacher education, and education curricula.

Four countries display horizontal bars representing their scores across four distinct educational strands in 2020, with values ranging from 0.56 to 1. The data shows that Colombia and India both achieve the maximum score of 1 in national education policies and student assessment. Colombia records 0.88 for education curricula and 0.85 for teacher education, while India scores 0.95 for teacher education and 0.92 for education curricula. Malawi also reaches the top value of 1 for national education policies and student assessment, followed by 0.91 for education curricula and 0.9 for teacher education. Bulgaria presents a different distribution, with teacher education at 0.73 and student assessment at 0.71, while education curricula sits at 0.65 and national education policies at 0.56. The indicators are based on self-reporting by countries, though nations are asked to provide supporting evidence to back up their responses. Three of the four nations cluster at the highest end of the scale for policy and assessment metrics, whereas Bulgaria shows lower values across all four categories compared to the other three. The visible pattern places national education policies and student assessment as the categories with the highest frequency of maximum scores among the displayed examples.
